result.note: LEARNED frame-interpolation spike: render every 2th frame fully, synthesise the in-betweens with model=flow_guided. flow_guided: Cycles ground-truth motion warps BOTH keyframes to the target time + occlusion-aware blend (hand-built refine, not a trained net) -> modeled:true. scene=animated (keyframed monkey+sphere+camera, REAL motion vectors), 8 frames @ 384x384 spp=256. net_speedup = full-render-ALL-frames wall-time / (keyframe-render wall-time + interpolation wall-time), ALL measured on this box (SSIM is measurement-only, NOT charged). quality = mean GLOBAL SSIM of the 3 synthesised frames vs their TRUE full Cycles renders; worst_tile_ssim / p5_tile_ssim are the min / 5th-pctile of an 8x8 per-tile SSIM grid (catch a blur that lifts the average). warp_baseline_ssim = the NAIVE single-keyframe forward-warp (the temporal runner's reproject) SSIM on the SAME frames — the ~0.84 ceiling we attack; beat_warp=True (learned/flow mean 0.8967 vs warp 0.8859). modeled:true — the interpolator's refine is a hand-built occlusion-aware flow blend (or the RIFE->flow fallback), NOT a trained network; every TIME and SSIM is still a real measurement, only the 'learned' label is withheld.
